game_name: Tiny Toon Adventures: Buster Busts Loose!

danger_signals.visual_form: Antagonistic cartoon characters (Duck Vader, destructive entities)
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat requiring avoidance or defeat

danger_signals.visual_form: Destructive scenarios (e.g., Dizzy Devil destroying school kitchen)

danger_signals.meaning: Increasing danger to player character; game-over risk

danger_signals.visual_form: Single health container (visual reduction from normal modes)
danger_signals.meaning: Maximum danger state; minimal margin for error

safety_signals.meaning: Safe resource acquisition; player resilience increases

safety_signals.visual_form: Wheel of Comedy spin at level completion
safety_signals.meaning: Safe respite state; chance to earn extra lives (resource abundance)

safety_signals.signal: Reduced level complexity in easy mode
safety_signals.visual_form: Smaller level layouts visually and spatially

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player-centric level progression; objectives tied to helping allies

faction_indicators.faction: Tiny Toons allies (Dizzy Devil, Hamton, Babs, Plucky Duck, etc.)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character appearances in minigames and cutscenes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Minigame wheel representations; roulette cutscene featuring specific character icons

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Duck Vader as primary antagonist visual
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Final level objective; represents culmination of threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental damage or destructive character presence in level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must feed Dizzy Devil to stop destruction; reactive problem-solving

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Children mode uses significantly smaller level layouts; Challenge mode reduces health container availability
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player movement and resource management constrained by world-state difficulty; fewer exploration options in easy mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ending screen varies by difficulty: Japanese version shows Babs (Children) or Buster (Normal); international versions unified with difficulty messages or 'The End'
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty-dependent narrative closure; encouragement to replay on harder difficulty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Credits animated with characters and backgrounds in higher difficulties (Challenge mode in international versions; Normal in Japanese)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Full narrative payload unlocked only at highest difficulty; visual ceremony marks world completion

environmental_storytelling.example: Each level presents a different problem requiring Buster's intervention (feeding Dizzy, defeating Duck Vader), communicating world conditions through objective design rather than explicit exposition
The Wheel O'Comedy roulette spin references the animated series and associates specific Tiny Toons characters with minigame types, embedding franchise lore into reward mechanics

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Different ending screens, credit sequences, and character appearances based on difficulty selected
environmental_storytelling.meaning: World state is conditional on player mastery level; harder difficulties unlock 'truer' narrative states

environmental_storytelling.example: Regional differences in password availability (Children difficulty only in US/EU) communicate which world-states are officially 'accessible' to players

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Side-scrolling locomotion, interaction with level hazards and objectives
Health container reduction; visual loss of resilience indicator
Movement constrained by single health container; heightened caution evident in play patterns
Actively destroying school kitchen; threat to world stability
Cessation of destructive action; world-state stabilized by player intervention
Boss-tier antagonist; culmination of escalating threat across level progression

notes: Tiny Toons Buster Busts Loose uses primarily mechanical and objective-based world-state communication rather than heavy environmental storytelling. Danger is communicated through health mechanics (single container in Challenge mode) and antagonist presence. Safety signals are resource-based (extra containers, minigame rewards). The game's manifestation layer relies heavily on difficulty modes to communicate world state—the same levels present different challenges visually through layout reduction and health scarcity. Regional differences (password availability, ending variations, animated credits in Challenge mode) indicate how world-state accessibility is culturally gated. The minigame roulette wheel and character associations embed faction identity through licensed IP; character appearances in specific minigames create faction-to-mechanic associations. No explicit color-coding or sigil-based faction systems are documented; instead, faction identity is carried through character sprite recognition and narrative context from the source material.
